description.task: Implement Huffman coding tree construction. Given characters and their frequencies, use a min-heap to build the optimal prefix-free code tree. Print the final Huffman codes for each character.
instructions: Node a=pq.poll(); Node b=pq.poll(); pq.add(new Node(a.freq+b.freq,a,b));
Frequencies: a=5,b=9,c=12,d=13,e=16,f=45; merge smallest pairs repeatedly
f(45) gets the shortest code (0 or 1 depending on merge order); a(5) gets the longest
printCodes is already implemented — just build the tree correctly
Huffman encoding minimizes total bits = sum(freq[i] * codeLength[i])

description.task: Extend Huffman coding with encode and decode functions. Build the code map from the Huffman tree, encode a message to a bit string, then decode the bit string back to the original message by traversing the tree.
instructions: msg='abracadabra': 11 chars; with Huffman codes, much fewer bits than 8*11=88 bits
The decoded string must match the original message exactly
buildMap and buildTree are already provided — only encode and decode need implementation
description.hint: encode: for(char c:msg.toCharArray())sb.append(map.get(c)); decode: cur=(bit=='0')?cur.left:cur.right; if leaf: sb.append(cur.ch);cur=root;
description.expectedOutput:
Codes: {a=0, b=111, r=110, c=1000, d=1001}
Encoded length: 23 bits
Decoded: abracadabra
Match: true

description.task: Calculate the compression ratio achieved by Huffman coding. Compare the number of bits required with Huffman vs fixed-width encoding (⌈log2(n)⌉ bits per symbol). Print the compression ratio and bits saved.
instructions: Huffman bits: compute using provided code lengths; typical result ~224
Compression ratio ≈ 74% of original size, saving about 76 bits
description.expectedOutput:
Total symbols: 100
Fixed-width bits/symbol: 3
Fixed-width total bits:  300
Huffman total bits:      224
Compression ratio: 74%
Bits saved: 76
